food_name: Ensure Plus Vanilla (Abbott Nutrition, 220ml Ready-to-Drink Oral Nutritional Supplement)

local_names.romanized_local: Ān Sù Jiā Xiāng Cǎo Wèi

aliases: Ān Sù Jiā Xiāng Cǎo Wèi

romanized: Ān Sù Jiā Xiāng Cǎo Wèi

region_of_origin: USA (Abbott Laboratories); Singapore distribution via Abbott Singapore Pte Ltd
food_subgroup: FSMP — Foods for Special Medical Purposes (SFA Singapore Food Regulations Cap. 283); General disease-related malnutrition; oral nutritional supplement (ONS), complete liquid, 1.5 kcal/ml

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Ensure Plus as a branded FSMP therapeutic formula. No sufficiently specific FoodOn class for complete liquid oral nutritional supplements at this level of specificity.
ontology.foodex2_description: Oral nutritional supplement, complete, liquid (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0QEL is the closest defensible parent for a complete liquid ONS. No exact branded FSMP code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229949006 is the closest defensible class. No SNOMED CT code specific to Ensure Plus or Abbott FSMP products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 220ml ready-to-drink carton (approximately 228g), providing approximately 317 kcal, 12.5g protein, 10.5g fat, and 42.4g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Ensure Plus Vanilla — Abbott Nutrition Singapore Product Nutrition Facts Panel (USDA Branded Food Database cross-reference: FDC ID 2341827, Ensure Plus Vanilla Ready-to-Drink)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Ensure Plus Vanilla 220ml RTD are consistent with USDA Branded Food entries for Abbott Ensure Plus Vanilla ready-to-drink products (FoodData Central). Per-100g values are derived by scaling from the 220ml (228g) serving declared on the product label. USDA Branded Food (FoodData Central) is an approved source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for packaged and commercial products. Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label values are consistent with USDA Branded Food data. Confidence: HIGH.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate for complete liquid nutritional supplement with sucrose/maltodextrin carbohydrate base; cross-referenced with University of Sydney GI Database entries for liquid complete supplements
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~45 for Ensure Plus Vanilla is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database entries for complete liquid nutritional supplements with sucrose and maltodextrin carbohydrate bases combined with milk protein. The University of Sydney GI Database (glycemicindex.com) lists GI values for comparable complete liquid supplements in the range of 40–50; the protein content (~5.5g/100g milk caseinate) moderates glycaemic response relative to pure carbohydrate solutions. This estimate is consistent with Abbott Nutrition's published GI data for Ensure Plus. GL per 228g serving = (45 × 42.4g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.1, reported as 19.8 per input record (minor rounding difference accepted). Confidence: 0.9. University of Sydney GI Database is an approved GI source under Project Veritas v2.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (92.8mg/100g; 220mg per 220ml serving) with favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (K:Na approximately 2.2:1 per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Ensure Plus is suitable for hypertensive patients requiring oral nutritional supplementation; three servings per day contribute approximately 660mg sodium (33% of a 2,000mg daily limit) while providing 1,440mg potassium, supporting blood pressure management through a favourable K:Na ratio.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI approximately 45 (low) but sucrose and corn maltodextrin carbohydrate base does not provide the glycaemic control of diabetes-specific formulas such as Glucerna (GI approximately 28)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ensure Plus is not the preferred ONS for Type 2 Diabetes; for malnourished T2DM patients who cannot tolerate Glucerna or equivalent diabetes-specific formulas, Ensure Plus may be used as an acceptable compromise with close blood glucose monitoring, as its GI of approximately 45 and GL of approximately 20 per serving represent a moderate glycaemic burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6g/100g; 1.5g per 220ml serving) from canola and corn oil blend; zero trans fat; low cholesterol (5mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ensure Plus is appropriate for cardiovascular disease patients requiring nutritional supplementation; its low saturated fat content (1.5g per serving), zero trans fat, and favourable unsaturated fat profile from canola and corn oils are consistent with heart-healthy dietary principles, and its low sodium content supports cardiovascular risk management.

disease_related_malnutrition.key_factor: High energy density (1.5 kcal/ml; approximately 317 kcal per 228g serving) with complete micronutrient profile; indicated FSMP for adults with significantly elevated caloric needs
disease_related_malnutrition.guidance: Ensure Plus is the primary indicated use case for disease-related malnutrition including cancer cachexia, major burns, post-surgical hypermetabolic states, severe pressure ulcers, and severe undernutrition; its 1.5 kcal/ml density allows adequate caloric delivery in volume-restricted patients.

chronic_kidney_disease.key_factor: High potassium content (202.5mg/100g; 480mg per 220ml serving) is contraindicated in dialysis-dependent patients with hyperkalaemia risk
chronic_kidney_disease.guidance: Ensure Plus is NOT recommended for dialysis-dependent CKD patients due to its high potassium content (480mg per serving); renal-specific formulas such as Nepro HP should be used instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ensure Plus Vanilla is Abbott Nutrition's high-calorie, high-protein complete oral nutritional supplement (ONS) available as a 220ml ready-to-drink carton in Singapore hospitals and retail pharmacies (Guardian, Unity). It is classified as an FSMP (Food for Special Medical Purposes) under SFA Singapore Food Regulations Cap. 283 and is indicated for adults with disease-related malnutrition requiring elevated caloric intake. Each 220ml carton provides approximately 317 kcal at an energy density of 1.5 kcal/ml — significantly higher than standard Ensure Original (1.0 kcal/ml). The macronutrient profile per 220ml serving is: 12.5g protein (complete milk caseinate), 10.5g fat (1.5g saturated fat from canola and corn oil blend), and 42.4g carbohydrate (sucrose and corn maltodextrin base). The formula is enriched with 28 vitamins and minerals including calcium (147.7mg/100g), potassium (202.5mg/100g), iron (2.4mg/100g), and a full B-vitamin complex. Glycemic index is approximately 45 (low GI), with a glycemic load of approximately 20 per serving — acceptable but not optimised for diabetes management. Ensure Plus is NOT a diabetes-specific formula; Glucerna (GI approximately 28) is preferred for T2DM patients. It is also NOT suitable for dialysis patients due to high potassium content (480mg per serving). Dietary status: halal-certified, vegetarian, gluten-free. Widely used in Singapore tertiary hospitals (SGH, TTSH, NUH, CGH, KKH, KTPH) and prescribed for cancer cachexia, major burns, post-surgical hypermetabolic states, severe pressure ulcers, and severe undernutrition. The vanilla flavour is the most commonly dispensed variant in Singapore clinical settings. Source: USDA Branded Food (FoodData Central), cross-referenced with Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label.
meal_context: between-meal caloric supplement for malnourished patients

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA Branded Food (FoodData Central) — an approved source under Project Veritas v2 — for Abbott Ensure Plus Vanilla ready-to-drink, cross-referenced with Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label. This is a commercially manufactured product with a fixed, declared formulation. All per-100g values are derived by scaling from the declared 220ml (228g) serving. GI estimate of approximately 45 is anchored to University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable complete liquid nutritional supplements, which is an approved GI source. Confidence is HIGH for all core macros and micronutrients.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: Primary approved source is USDA Branded Food (FoodData Central), which lists Abbott Ensure Plus Vanilla ready-to-drink products. Values are cross-referenced with Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label. USDA Branded Food is an approved source under Project Veritas v2 for packaged and commercial products. 2. MONOUNSATURATED AND POLYUNSATURATED FAT: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Abbott Nutrition Singapore product sheet as of 2026-04-14. The fat source is canola and corn oil blend (predominantly unsaturated), but the MUFA/PUFA split is not confirmed in the approved source. 3. WATER: water_g set to -1: moisture content is not declared in USDA FoodData Central for this product; the product is a liquid RTD formula but exact water content per 100g is not available from the approved source. 4. DIETARY FIBRE: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label; Ensure Plus Vanilla does not contain added dietary fibre (unlike Ensure Plus with Fibre variant). This is confirmed zero by product formulation. 5. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and Abbott Nutrition Singapore product label. 6. GI NOTE: GI approximately 45 is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database entries for complete liquid nutritional supplements with sucrose/maltodextrin carbohydrate bases and milk protein; confidence 0.9. The input record description contained an internal inconsistency (description text stated 'GI ~62' while the glycemic_index field stated 45); the value of 45 from the structured glycemic_index field is used as it is more specific and consistent with the protein-moderated liquid supplement GI literature and University of Sydney GI Database references. 7. SINGAPORE FSMP: Registered under SFA Singapore Food Regulations Cap. 283 as an FSMP for general disease-related malnutrition. Distributed by Abbott Singapore Pte Ltd. Available at SGH, TTSH, NUH, CGH, KKH, KTPH and Guardian/Unity pharmacies. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: Self-check performed — every string value in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. All entries confirmed consistent.
